intended_use: Location-aware HVAC guidance for contractors, builders, architects, and homeowners across U.S. regions.

units.notes: Design ranges are practical regional bands (not city-specific). Always confirm local ASHRAE design conditions and AHJ requirements.

always_verify: Local design temperatures (ASHRAE/Manual J tables and local weather files)
Local energy code edition and amendments (IECC/ASHRAE 90.1 or state equivalents)
AHJ permitting, commissioning/testing requirements, and documentation
Utility program requirements (rebates often require specific test results and model numbers)
common_best_practices: Manual J load calculations (room-by-room), Manual S equipment selection, Manual D duct design
Measured airflow and static pressure verification at startup
Duct leakage testing where required and always for quality assurance
Combustion safety and CO testing for fuel-burning appliances
Moisture management: airtightness, ventilation strategy, and correct latent capacity

coverage.notes: Hot-humid, long cooling season, high latent loads; coastal corrosion risk.

hvac_system_considerations.sizing_notes: Prioritize latent removal; avoid oversizing that short-cycles and leaves humidity high.
hvac_system_considerations.dehumidification_needs: High - whole-house dehumidifiers strongly recommended for tight envelopes
hvac_system_considerations.equipment_protection: Coastal coating on coils mandatory within 5 miles of coast. Hurricane straps. Flood elevation.

pro_tips: Comfort is a humidity project disguised as a temperature project.
Attic ducts are performance kryptonite; every seal and inch of insulation pays rent.
Condensate safety switches save ceilings and reputations.
Install float switches on ALL units.

coverage.notes: Hot-dry, large day/night swings, intense solar gain; dust and monsoon bursts.

recommended_system_types: High-efficiency heat pump or AC + furnace
hvac_system_considerations.sizing_notes: Solar gain drives peak loads. Manual J must account for high sensible heat ratio.
hvac_system_considerations.dehumidification_needs: Rarely needed. Humidifiers often requested for comfort.
hvac_system_considerations.equipment_protection: Sun shields for rooftop units. Hail guards. Dust management.

common_regional_problems.maintenance_priority: Wash outdoor coils monthly in summer. Check capacitors annually.

pro_tips: Shade the condenser like it's a vampire but keep airflow like it's training for a marathon.
Dust is the silent compressor killer.
Use hard start kits on compressors for high head pressure.
Don't set setback thermostats too high; recovery at 110°F is impossible.

coverage.notes: Cold winters, humid summers, older housing stock; retrofits common.

hvac_system_considerations.sizing_notes: Heat load dominates. Ensure heat pump capacity at low ambient matches load.
hvac_system_considerations.dehumidification_needs: Basement dehumidifiers standard. Winter humidification often needed.
hvac_system_considerations.equipment_protection: Snow stands (12-18" risers). Ice shields for roofs above units. Freeze protection.

top_issues: Heat pump underperformance in extreme cold
common_regional_problems.maintenance_priority: Combustion analysis and filter changes before winter.
common_regional_problems.mold_risk: Moderate - basements in summer, attics in winter

pro_tips: Basements are the Northeast's humidity factory. Treat them like part of the system.
Never install a heat pump directly on the ground; it will become an ice block.
Steam radiators provide excellent comfort - add mini-splits for cooling instead of replacing.
Variable speed fans reduce the 'cold blow' feeling vs oil heat.

coverage.notes: Extreme annual temperature swing; cold winters, hot humid summers.

hvac_system_considerations.sizing_notes: Design for both extremes. Wind infiltration is major load factor.
hvac_system_considerations.dehumidification_needs: Recommended for basements. HRVs preferred over ERVs in far north.
hvac_system_considerations.equipment_protection: Snow clearance. Freeze protection for condensate. Wind protection.

common_regional_problems.maintenance_priority: Carbon monoxide checks non-negotiable every fall.

pro_tips: Midwest comfort is a two-season chess match. Win both by nailing airflow and envelope leakage.
High static pressure quietly wrecks efficiency. Measure it, don't guess.
90%+ furnaces: Insulate condensate lines in unconditioned spaces or they freeze.
During polar vortex, keep thermostats constant; setbacks can't recover.

coverage.notes: Mild temps, long damp season; growing cooling needs; wildfire smoke IAQ concerns.

seasonality: Summer: drier but smoke dominates IAQ

hvac_system_considerations.sizing_notes: Historic homes often lack ductwork. Consider emerging heat-wave peaks.
hvac_system_considerations.dehumidification_needs: Low to moderate - focus on damp-season mold prevention.
hvac_system_considerations.equipment_protection: Moss/algae control. Rain drainage. UV tape for exterior line sets.

top_issues: Lack of cooling in legacy homes
common_regional_problems.maintenance_priority: Clean blower wheels - dust sticks to damp blades.
common_regional_problems.mold_risk: High - 'sick building syndrome' common in older damp homes

pro_tips: Design for the 'smoke week' and the 'heat wave week' even if they're not most weeks.
Customers are new to AC here; explain heat pumps blow 'cool' warm air vs furnaces.
Install fresh air intakes with dampers that can close during smoke events.
Damp-season mold comes from low runtime - ventilation strategy matters more than cooling.

coverage.notes: Highly diverse microclimates; Title 24 is most stringent energy code in US.

central_valley.climate: Hot dry summer (100°F+), Tule fog winter
central_valley.focus: High cooling efficiency, air filtration for ag dust

desert.focus: Peak cooling, dust management, condenser shading

mountain.focus: Heating reliability, snow management, backup heat

regional_code_notes.details: Requires HERS testing (duct leakage, refrigerant charge, airflow) for almost all alterations.
regional_code_notes.refrigerant: Aggressive low-GWP phase-down leading the nation.
regional_code_notes.electrification: Many municipalities banning new gas hookups.

pro_tips: If you touch ductwork in CA, you trigger a HERS test. Quote accordingly.
Variable speed equipment almost mandatory for Title 24 EER requirements.
In mild coastal zones, comfort is airflow and control logic, not raw tonnage.
Wildfire smoke mode: iWave or similar + high MERV media cabinets.

coverage.notes: Cold winters, dry air, altitude impacts; snow management central.

hvac_system_considerations.sizing_notes: ALTITUDE DERATING: Gas appliances lose ~4% capacity per 1,000ft elevation.
hvac_system_considerations.dehumidification_needs: Not needed. ADD humidifiers (steam preferred over bypass).
hvac_system_considerations.equipment_protection: Snow hoods for intakes/exhausts. Hail guards. Wind protection.

top_issues: Insufficient combustion air in tight homes
common_regional_problems.maintenance_priority: Check gas pressure manifold settings against altitude charts.

pro_tips: You MUST change pressure switches on many furnaces above 5,000ft.
Don't trust rating plate BTU output - calculate derated output for Manual J.
Radiant floor heating is premium standard in luxury mountain homes.
Altitude turns 'normal' commissioning numbers into lies if you don't adjust.
Outdoor unit placement is half HVAC and half snow science.

coverage.notes: Wide climate diversity; ERCOT grid concerns; resilience planning critical.

gulf_coast.common_systems: Variable speed heat pumps with dehumidifiers
gulf_coast.key_issue: Humidity is the boss, temperature is the employee

central.climate: Very hot summers, occasional hard freezes (Austin, Dallas)
central.focus: Peak cooling, hail protection, freeze resilience
central.key_issue: Attic ducts can be hotter than your future if ignored

panhandle.key_issue: Wind is basically another occupant - plan infiltration like it pays rent

regional_code_notes.details: Code adoption varies wildly. Austin/Dallas strict; rural areas little enforcement.
regional_code_notes.grid: ERCOT instability drives demand for soft-start kits and generator integration.

pro_tips: In Houston, if you don't seal the plenum perfectly, it will sweat and ruin the ceiling.
Critter guards essential in rural Texas - ants love contactors, mice love wires.
Surge protectors mandatory due to lightning and grid fluctuations.

coverage.notes: Marine climate; salt corrosion primary concern; high electricity costs.

hvac_system_considerations.sizing_notes: Cooling only. Heating strips rarely needed.
hvac_system_considerations.dehumidification_needs: Moderate - humidity persistent, mold risk depends on ventilation.
hvac_system_considerations.equipment_protection: EVERYTHING must be coated. Boards need conformal coating. Copper with coating only.

common_regional_problems.maintenance_priority: Rinse outdoor units with fresh water MONTHLY.

pro_tips: The ocean is basically an unpaid technician trying to dissolve your condenser.
Coil washing cadence is not optional - it's a lifestyle.
Put mothballs or repellent in electrical compartments to keep geckos out.
Solar integration huge due to $0.40+/kWh rates.
Aluminum line sets dissolve here - copper with coating only.

coverage.notes: Extreme cold, long heating season, remote service constraints; moisture control critical.

seasonality: Condensation control is building durability priority

hvac_system_considerations.sizing_notes: Redundancy is life-safety. You NEED a backup heat source. Plan for freeze protection everywhere.
hvac_system_considerations.dehumidification_needs: Low. Primary goal is preventing condensation from indoor moisture.
hvac_system_considerations.equipment_protection: Fuel tanks must be indoors or have heat tape. Raised stands. Wind breaks.

common_regional_problems.maintenance_priority: Fuel filter changes. Vent clearance. HRV core maintenance.

pro_tips: Moisture is a structural enemy. Comfort comes second to keeping the building dry.
A blocked vent termination can become full system failure. Protect terminations like critical infrastructure.
In permafrost areas, heat loss can melt the foundation - HVAC design involves keeping floor cold.
Air sealing is more important than R-value to prevent moisture drive into walls.
Keep winter RH at 25-35% to prevent condensation damage.
